title: Explainability Capstone
concept: Explainability is essential for building human trust, debugging bias, and ensuring safety in high-stakes vision applications.

exercises.prompt: Recap: How does Grad-CAM help detect shortcut learning?

options.text: It highlights whether the model is looking at the target object or relying on background features
It automatically removes noise from the training images
It measures the execution latency of the model on edge devices

exercises.explain: Grad-CAM maps reveal the semantic areas the model focused on, exposing any background shortcuts.

cards.title: Trust, but Verify
cards.body: As computer vision models enter high-stakes domains like medicine, self-driving cars, and national security, explainability stops being a cool diagnostic tool and becomes a safety requirement. We must build AI that can explain its actions to the humans who rely on it.

cards.title: Responsible AI
cards.body: Responsible AI is a framework for developing machine learning systems that are fair, transparent, accountable, and safe. Model Explainability serves as the auditing tool that checks models for bias, shortcuts, and safety failures before they can cause harm.

cards.title: A Doctor's explanation
cards.body: When a doctor diagnoses you, they don't just say 'take this pill'. They explain why: they show you the X-ray, point to the shadow, and explain how the medicine targets it. High-stakes AI must explain itself in the same way to build trust with professionals.

cards.title: Surgical Verification
cards.body: In robotic surgery, a model detects anatomical boundaries. Before cutting, the system displays a Grad-CAM overlay of the target tissue on the surgeon's monitor. The surgeon verifies the model's focus, ensuring the machine hasn't locked onto a metal clamp.

cards.title: Explainability is not just for coders
cards.body: You might think only developers care about saliency heatmaps. In reality, end-users (like doctors, insurance auditors, and truck drivers) rely on explainability tools to verify predictions, override incorrect decisions, and satisfy legal audits.

cards.title: The Audited AI Pipeline
cards.body: How explainability integrates into a responsible deployment workflow:

left.text:
Model outputs predictions blindly.
Spurious biases remain hidden.
High risk of real-world failure & liability.

right.text:
Predictions paired with heatmaps.
Biases are caught and corrected.
Builds professional trust & safety.

exercises.prompt: Why is explainability considered a requirement in high-stakes fields like medicine?
exercises.explain: In high-stakes tasks, human verification of model reasoning is critical to protect lives and maintain trust.

options.text: It allows human specialists (like doctors) to verify the model's clinical reasoning, ensuring safety and accountability
It speeds up the processing frames-per-second on mobile phones
It automatically converts grayscale images to color
It eliminates the need for any training labels

exercises.prompt: True or False: Responsible AI frameworks rely on explainability tools to audit models for bias and shortcut learning.
exercises.explain: True. Saliency maps and Grad-CAM are standard tools used to check and verify that models make predictions for valid reasons.
Transparency through visual explanations builds professional and user trust in AI predictions.
exercises.sentence: Pairing a model's prediction with a visual heatmap explanation helps build user ___.

exercises.prompt: Which group of people benefits from model explainability features?
exercises.explain: Explainability helps developers debug shortcuts and gives domain experts the context needed to trust decisions.

options.text: Both developers debugging models and end-users (like doctors and auditors) verifying predictions
Only the raw GPU hardware layers that execute convolutional arithmetic
Only the automated labeling scrapers that collect raw images

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a self-driving truck. It scores 99% on driving test tracks. Why must you still run explainability audits during verification?

options.text: To verify that the model is making steering decisions based on road markings and vehicles rather than spurious track-specific background details.
To reduce the truck's fuel consumption.
To convert the camera feeds to YOLO text format.
To ensure the truck only drives on straight paths.

exercises.explain: Explainability audits ensure the model is reacting to actual road entities, avoiding dangerous mistakes when it encounters new environments. Phase 12: Vision Data & Evaluation Deep Dive is complete. Next up: Video & Temporal Vision.
